2025-06-10·2 min read

By Random US Address Generator Team

State Capitals Of The United States

Each US state has a capital city where its government is headquartered. These capitals range from major metropolitan areas like Phoenix, Arizona, with over 1.6 million residents, to small towns like Montpelier, Vermont, with fewer than 10,000 residents. All 50 state capitals serve as the seat of state government, housing the governor's office, the state legislature, and the state supreme court in most cases. The geographic and demographic diversity of these capitals makes them excellent candidates for generating varied test addresses that cover a wide spectrum of population densities and urban layouts. From the dense urban environment of Boston to the sprawling desert city of Phoenix, each capital presents unique address patterns, ZIP code distributions, and area code assignments that developers should account for when building location-aware software.

The list of state capitals includes some of America's best-known cities. Boston is the capital of Massachusetts, Atlanta is the capital of Georgia, Denver is the capital of Colorado, and Austin is the capital of Texas. Other notable capitals include Sacramento (California), Albany (New York), Columbus (Ohio), Indianapolis (Indiana), Nashville (Tennessee), and Olympia (Washington). Some capitals are less well-known but equally important: Pierre is the capital of South Dakota, Bismarck is the capital of North Dakota, Helena is the capital of Montana, and Juneau is the capital of Alaska. Each capital has unique address patterns that reflect its size and geographic region.

Each state capital has its own ZIP code and area code. Phoenix uses ZIP codes starting with 850 and area code 602. Austin uses ZIP codes starting with 787 and area code 512. Albany uses ZIP codes starting with 122 and area code 518. These geographic identifiers are important for generating realistic addresses that include valid city, state, and telephone data. You can browse ZIP codes for any capital city using our ZIP code directory. Our random address generator includes addresses from all 50 state capitals, making it easy to generate realistic addresses for government and administrative testing scenarios.

For developers who need to test address forms that handle government or administrative addresses, our state-specific generator pages provide addresses from all cities within each state, including the capital. This allows you to test how your application handles addresses from both the capital city and other cities within the same state. If you need addresses focused on a particular capital or major city, our city address generator lets you drill down to specific municipalities. Our test address generator also produces a broader variety of addresses that can help ensure your application correctly validates and processes government-related address data across all 50 states. Knowing the capital of each state is also helpful for understanding regional address patterns, ZIP code assignments, and area code distributions that vary significantly between densely populated capitals like Phoenix and rural ones like Montpelier. Whether you are building government applications, logistics software, or general-purpose address forms, having accurate capital city data ensures your test addresses reflect real geographic and administrative divisions.
